title: Design a Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack for a real-time inference service. What exact metrics would you expose from the model server?
prompt:
Design a Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack for a real-time inference service. What exact metrics would you expose from the model server?

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
I'd expose the standard golden signals plus ML-specific ones, deliberately separated in the dashboard layout. Infra layer: request latency as a histogram, request/error counts labeled by status code and model version, requests in flight, queue depth, and resource metrics. Model-quality layer: prediction score distribution as a histogram, per-model-version prediction volume, and feature computation latency tracked separately from model inference latency. Separating infra from model-quality metrics means on-call can glance at the dashboard and immediately know whether they're dealing with an infrastructure problem or a model behavior problem.

**Takeaway:** Use histograms over summaries for latency since Prometheus histograms aggregate percentiles server-side across a fleet, and never label by user_id or request_id since that causes cardinality explosion.
hint_notes: Two dashboard layers: infra (latency histogram, error counts by version, queue depth) and model-quality (prediction score distribution, per-version volume, feature-vs-inference latency split). Use histograms; never label by user_id/request_id.
